oneLineSummary: A child-friendly sustainability learning concept exploring visual communication, simple interaction patterns, and audience-aware educational design.

summary.systemDefinition: A concept project focused on early sustainability learning for younger audiences.
coreWorkflowsFeatures: Simple interaction patterns for young learners
Visual communication tailored to child-friendly understanding
Educational experience framed through research and accessibility thinking
summary.businessOrOperationalImpact: The concept examines how sustainability topics can be made more approachable and engaging for children.
summary.engineeringValue: It demonstrates research-led UX thinking, educational framing, and careful audience-aware design decisions.

projectNarrative.problem: Sustainability education often uses explanations that are too abstract or visually dense for younger children.
projectNarrative.approach: I explored a more playful, simplified learning concept that prioritises accessible communication and audience-appropriate interaction.
projectNarrative.result: The project reinforced the importance of visual clarity and research-backed simplification in educational design.

reflection.tradeoffs: Keeping the concept simple enough for children meant removing interactions that were visually appealing but cognitively distracting.
lessonsLearned: Educational concepts need age-appropriate abstraction levels.
Research can guide which playful elements truly support learning.

display.libraryFocus: playful communication, audience-aware design, and educational usability
